name: Option Alpha
description_short: Options trading automation platform with visual bot builder, backtesting, SmartPricing, and extensive free education for systematic options sellers.
description_long:
## What Option Alpha Actually Is

Option Alpha is an options trading automation platform built for systematic options sellers who want to trade rules-based strategies without babysitting screens all day. The company was founded in 2015 by Kirk Du Plessis, a former Wall Street M&A analyst who built a following through his free options education podcast and blog before turning the brand into a full-fledged software company. It's headquartered in Richmond, Virginia and operates as Option Alpha LLC.

The pitch is simple: you build trading bots using a visual, no-code interface, backtest them against historical options data, then let them run in a live brokerage account. It sits in the options-platforms category alongside tools like tastytrade and OptionStrat, but Option Alpha is more focused than either — it exists almost entirely to help you sell premium in a repeatable, automated way.

## Core Features and What Makes It Different

The headline feature is the **visual bot builder**. Instead of writing code in Python or a proprietary scripting language, you drag decision blocks and rules into a flowchart-style canvas: entry conditions, position sizing, profit targets, stop losses, adjustment logic, exit rules. Once the bot is built, it monitors the market during trading hours and executes trades through your connected broker without you touching anything.

**SmartPricing** is Option Alpha's answer to bad fills on multi-leg options orders. Rather than sending an order at the mid and hoping, SmartPricing works the order aggressively across the bid-ask spread using a stepping algorithm, which in practice tends to fill closer to mid than a naive limit order. For anyone who's watched a four-legged iron condor sit unfilled for twenty minutes, this alone is meaningful.

**Backtesting** uses historical options chain data so you can test a strategy's rules across years of market conditions before risking capital. You can test variations of the same strategy — different delta targets, different DTEs, different profit-taking rules — and compare results side by side. The **Trade Ideas** feature ranks setups by expected value, giving you a curated starting point rather than a blank screen.

Rounding it out: a genuinely large free education library (200+ videos covering options mechanics, strategy design, and platform usage), an active community, paper trading, and mobile app access.

## Pricing Breakdown

Option Alpha runs three tiers. The **Free** plan gets you the 200+ education videos, community access, paper trading, and basic position tracking — no bots, but a real onboarding path. Notably, the site also advertises a free upgrade if you connect a TradeStation or Tradier brokerage account, so if you're already at one of those brokers, you may be able to skip paying entirely for a while.

**Starter** runs $39/month or $348/year (about $29/mo annual), and includes one automation bot, SmartPricing, position management, and basic backtesting. This is enough to run a single strategy live, which is honestly all most beginners should be doing anyway.

**Pro** is $99/month or $948/year (about $79/mo annual). You get unlimited bots, advanced backtesting, SmartPricing, priority support, and multi-broker connections. There's also a periodically-advertised sale (the July 4th promo runs 72% off), so if you're on the fence, waiting for a sale is rational.

Pricing tiers may shift, so verify current numbers on their site before subscribing.

## Honest Pros

**The bot builder actually works for non-programmers.** A lot of "no-code" tools in trading are marketing fiction — you still need to understand loops and conditionals. Option Alpha's visual interface is genuinely usable by someone who has never written code.

**SmartPricing is a real edge on execution.** Fill quality on multi-leg options orders is a hidden tax most retail traders never quantify. Getting closer to mid over hundreds of trades a year compounds meaningfully.

**The free education is not a lead magnet dressed up as a course.** The 200+ videos cover real content — Greeks, volatility, strategy construction, position adjustment — at a depth most paid competitors don't match.

**Backtesting with historical options data.** Options backtesting is hard because most retail platforms don't have clean historical chains. Option Alpha does, and you can iterate quickly on rule variations.

**Community and support are active.** The forums and Discord are not ghost towns, and the support team responds to actual questions rather than routing you through a chatbot maze.

## Honest Cons

**It only does one thing.** Option Alpha is built for options sellers running premium-collection strategies — iron condors, credit spreads, strangles, cash-secured puts, covered calls. If you want to buy calls on breakouts, trade earnings volatility as a buyer, or do anything directional, this is the wrong tool. Full stop.

**Broker integrations are narrow.** Currently Tradier, TradeStation, and Interactive Brokers are the main supported brokers. If you're at Schwab (post-thinkorswim), Fidelity, Robinhood, or Webull, you cannot use Option Alpha's automation with your existing account. You'd need to open a new brokerage.

**Pro at $99/month is a real commitment.** For it to make sense you need to be actively running multiple bots with enough capital that the automation saves you more than the fee. A $10k account running one strategy does not need Pro.

**Backtesting has known limits.** Historical options data can't perfectly capture liquidity, slippage in stressed markets, or fills you would or wouldn't have gotten. Backtested returns will almost always be optimistic vs. live results.

**No custom indicators or external data.** If your edge comes from a proprietary signal, custom indicator, or external data source, you can't easily wire it in. Option Alpha's rule set is opinionated, and you build inside that box.

## Who Should Use Option Alpha

Good fit: systematic options sellers who want to run mechanical strategies without discretion, options beginners who value structured education alongside a practical tool, working professionals who cannot watch screens during market hours, and traders already at Tradier, TradeStation, or IBKR who want to automate strategies they already run manually.

Bad fit: directional traders, day traders trading equities or futures, anyone who buys options as their primary approach, quants who want raw API access and custom code (Option Alpha has no public API), and traders with less than roughly $5k–$10k of options capital, where subscription fees eat too large a percentage of returns.

## How It Compares

**Option Alpha vs. tastytrade:** tastytrade is a broker with excellent options tools and free education, but it's a manual platform — no automated bots, no visual rule builder. If you want to click every trade yourself, tastytrade is cheaper (commissions only) and the education is comparable. If you want automation, only Option Alpha does it.

**Option Alpha vs. OptionStrat:** OptionStrat is primarily a visualization and analysis tool — payoff diagrams, unusual options activity, strategy screening. It's fantastic for planning a trade but has no automation and limited backtesting depth for rules-based systems. They solve different problems; many traders use both.

**Option Alpha vs. thinkorswim:** thinkorswim (now under Schwab) has thinkScript for custom studies and Conditional Orders for basic automation, but building a real rules-based bot with position management is painful. Option Alpha wins on automation ergonomics; thinkorswim wins on charting, market breadth, and being free with a Schwab account.

## The Bottom Line

Option Alpha is a specialist tool that's excellent at exactly what it's designed for and useless outside that lane. If you sell options premium systematically and want to stop staring at screens, it's arguably the best purpose-built platform on the market at any price. The visual bot builder is legitimately good, SmartPricing improves real-world execution, and the free education tier alone is worth exploring even if you never subscribe.

Where it falls short is scope. Non-options traders should ignore it. Options buyers and directional traders will find the toolset actively hostile to their style. And the $99/month Pro tier only makes economic sense once you're running enough automated capital that the fee is trivial compared to what your bots are earning.

Start with the free tier, run paper trades, and if you're already at Tradier or TradeStation, take advantage of the free connected-account offer before paying anything. Move to Starter when you're ready to run one live bot. Upgrade to Pro only when your single bot is profitable enough that you want to scale to multiple strategies.
pros: Visual no-code bot builder that non-programmers can actually use
SmartPricing algorithm improves fill quality on multi-leg orders
200+ free educational videos that go deeper than typical lead magnets
Historical options chain data enables real backtesting of rules
Free upgrade available when connecting TradeStation or Tradier
cons: Only useful for options selling — no directional or buying strategies supported
Broker support limited to Tradier, TradeStation, and Interactive Brokers
Pro plan at $99/month is steep for smaller accounts
No public API or custom indicator support for proprietary signals
Backtested results tend to overstate real-world live performance
best_for: Working professionals who cannot watch screens intraday
Options beginners who want structured education plus practical tools
Traders already at Tradier, TradeStation, or Interactive Brokers
Iron condor, credit spread, and cash-secured put traders
scraped_features: Trade Ideas ranked by expected value
Strategy template library from top backtests
unique_selling_points: Free access for TradeStation and Tradier users (no paid subscription needed)
0DTE Oracle uses backtesting against 1 year of intraday data to validate 0DTE trades instead of relying on probability estimates
Trade Ideas uses proprietary Expected Value (EV) and Alpha metrics to rank opportunities from thousands of position setups
Complete user control over bot behavior — no automated trading without explicit permission
Educational focus — includes Option Alpha Fast Track onboarding program
meta_title: Option Alpha Review 2026: Honest Take on Options Bots
meta_description: Option Alpha review 2026: visual options bot builder, SmartPricing, backtesting, and pricing from $39/mo. Honest pros, cons, and who should skip it.
company_background: Option Alpha was founded in 2015 by Kirk Du Plessis, a former Wall Street M&A analyst who built an early following through a free options education podcast and blog. The company, Option Alpha LLC, is headquartered in Richmond, Virginia and focuses exclusively on systematic options selling automation.
